author: Austin Crawford
title: W.O.W. Extravaganzas
summary: Come be a part of the weekly free clothing exchange for Block Island families. Clothes of every size, shape, age, and gender are welcome. Drop off clothes to donate and pick up something to take home, all items free. Located in the basement of the Saint Andrew Parish Center on Spring Street. Wednesdays, March 2, March 9, March 16. Contact Amy Doran, 401-632-7308 for more information. Volunteers are always welcome.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Farmers Market
summary: The Block Island Farmers Market is a must-visit!
content:
The Block Island Farmers Market is a must-visit!

Every Wednesday and Saturday from 9:00 am to 11:30 am at Legion Park, find yourself among numerous stands and tables offering a wide selection of goodies spanning from beautiful handmade jewelry and tasty treats to fresh-picked flower bouquets and fine-carved cutting boards. Try a freshly squeezed lemonade.

Meet local islanders and learn about their trade or craft while browsing and finding the perfect memento or gift to bring back home.

meta.title: Block Island Farmers Market
meta.description: Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.

Every Wednesday and Saturday from 9:00 am to 11:30 am at Legion Park, find yourself among numerous stands and tables offering a wide selection of goodies spanning from beautiful handmade jewelry, handmade lemonade to fresh-picked flower bouquets and fine-carved cutting boards.

Meet local islanders and learn about their trade or craft while browsing and finding the perfect memento or gift to bring back home.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Block Island Triathlon
summary: Sure, there’s plenty of swimming, biking, and running on Block Island. The question is, do you like to fit all 3 in before lunch or take your time and do them all over the course of a weekend? Or a summer?
content:
Sure, there’s plenty of swimming, biking, and running on Block Island. The question is, do you like to fit all 3 in before lunch or take your time and do them all over the course of a weekend? Or a summer?

If you love the smell of adrenaline in the morning, why not join about 400 other participants in the annual Block Island Triathlon Saturday, August 7th  at 9 am?

Start off at Fred Benson Town Beach with a brisk quarter-mile swim through the waters on the eastern side of the island. Take a second to drip dry, then buckle on your helmet for a 12-mile ride along the beautiful western coast. Trade your bike shoes for your running shoes and finish up on the eastern beaches with a 4-mile run. And if all that sounds completely crazy to you, just amble on down and enjoy watching the race with your coffee and donut at your side. 

Either way, it’s another fine Saturday morning on Block Island. For participants or spectators coming on the day of, you’ll have to catch the special 6 am Point Judith ferry added for this event. To pre-register, visit www.active.com. To find out more, call the Recreation Department at 401-466-3223.

meta.title: Block Island Triathlon
meta.description: The Block Island Triathlon will be on August 4, 2018.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Last Farmers Market (for the season) at the Historical Society
summary: Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.
content:
Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.

If a drizzle of local honey on a fresh-baked scone is your idea of heaven, you can find heaven on earth on Wednesday and Saturday mornings from 9 to 11:30 am at the Block Island Farmers' Market. (Word to the wise, come early before the scone lady sells out.)

With its many fruits and veggies, artisan cheese spreads, salsas, shellfish and more, the Market is a veritable festival for foodies. 

 

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Fall Zoom Nature Series with Kim
summary: Charlestown Beach
content:
Charlestown Beach

Kim Gaffett, The Nature Conservancy’s OVF Naturalist, will be hosting the fall season of Zoom on Nature. Each month Kim will take us on an annotated tour of one of the Island’s many natural areas, chosen with the beauty of the season in mind.

content:
Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.

If a drizzle of local honey on a fresh-baked scone is your idea of heaven, you can find heaven on earth on Wednesday and Saturday mornings from 9 to 11:30 am at the Block Island Farmers' Market. (Word to the wise, come early before the scone lady sells out.)

With its many fruits and veggies, artisan cheese spreads, salsas, shellfish and more, the Market is a veritable festival for foodies. 

Find us at Legion Park on the West Side.

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

title: Island Maritime Institute (BIMI)
summary: Your kids are going to LOVE the touch tanks!
content:
Your kids are going to LOVE the touch tanks!

Please call 401-500-3501 between 8 and 10 am any day to reserve a spot.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Block Island Basketball Camp
summary: Registration will be Monday morning July 13th beginning at 8:30 am, but we will need a firm commitment via email ASAP to ensure the we can follow the camp size limits set forth by the State.  The cost of the camp is $130 per child. Please let us know if you would like to register one or more campers.
content: Registration will be Monday morning July 13th beginning at 8:30 am, but we will need a firm commitment via email ASAP to ensure the we can follow the camp size limits set forth by the State.  The cost of the camp is $130 per child. Please let us know if you would like to register one or more campers.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Farmers Market at Legion Park
summary: Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.
content:
Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.

If a drizzle of local honey on a fresh-baked scone is your idea of heaven, you can find heaven on earth on Wednesday and Saturday mornings from 9 to 11:30 am at the Block Island Farmers' Market. (Word to the wise, come early before the scone lady sells out.)

With its many fruits and veggies, artisan cheese spreads, salsas, shellfish and more, the Market is a veritable festival for foodies. But wait, there's more. Need something to drink, try Lynn's Homemade Lemonade for some wholesome goodness.

Find us at Legion Park on the West Side.

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

meta.title: Farmers Market
meta.description: Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Farmers Market at the Solviken Property
summary: Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.
content:
Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.

If a drizzle of local honey on a fresh-baked scone is your idea of heaven, you can find heaven on earth on Wednesday and Saturday mornings from 9 to 11:30 am at the Block Island Farmers' Market. (Word to the wise, come early before the scone lady sells out.)

With its many fruits and veggies, artisan cheese spreads, salsas, shellfish and more, the Market is a veritable festival for foodies. But wait, there's more. Not only are there plenty of edibles to delight your taste buds, there are also arts and crafts and music and kids and dogs and a laid-back community atmosphere that makes you smile just being there. 

 

 

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

author: Robert Lucier
title: Norman Riker's Soccer Camp
content:
Camp will be broken up as follows:

Team Sofia ages 5 &6

9am, 10 am and 11 am

 Team Isabella ages 7 to 9

9am, 10 am and 11 am

Team Norman ages 10 to 14

9am, 10 am and 11 am
